methodology.source: reported outcomes from r/collegeresults and student submissions; institutional waitlist data from Common Data Set filings
methodology.limitations: many top schools do not publish waitlist outcomes; off-waitlist admit rates below are computed from admitted / offered and understate the real rate (not all offered accept the waitlist)

aggregate.notes: Brown, Michigan, and Northwestern publish admitted but not offered counts. UCLA, UChicago, Harvard, and Columbia do not publish C2 waitlist data at all.

carousel.headline: Waitlists are the admissions gap nobody tracks.
carousel.sub: We're building the first public record. Class of 2030 data is being collected now.

carousel.headline: The top 10 waitlisted schools in our data
carousel.sub: Last 2 cycles. Waitlisted students, all reported.

carousel.headline: Cornell offered 6,166 waitlist spots last year.
Yale took 0 off the waitlist.
carousel.sub: Dartmouth too. From 2,500+ combined waitlisted students.

carousel.headline: Every school's waitlist works differently.
carousel.sub: Some take hundreds. Some take none. Most never publish it.

carousel.headline: Michigan admitted 973 off its waitlist last year.
carousel.sub: That's more than Cornell, Stanford, Brown, JHU, Northwestern, Princeton, UPenn, MIT, Yale, and Dartmouth combined (706).

carousel.headline: Knowing which waitlists actually move matters.
carousel.sub: Your statistical reality is very different at Cornell than at Yale.

carousel.headline: How we got these numbers
carousel.sub: Student waitlists: 232 submissions from 2024-25 and 2025-26, sourced from r/collegeresults and on-site submissions. Institutional numbers: 12 schools' most recent Common Data Set filings, scraped directly from their institutional research pages. Some schools (UCLA, Harvard, UChicago, Columbia) do not publish waitlist data.

carousel.headline: If you are waitlisted, log it.
carousel.sub: When you hear back, update it. Help build the first public waitlist tracker.

scenes.voiceover: Two hundred and thirty-two seniors were waitlisted last cycle. None of them know how it ended.
Waitlists are the biggest gap in college admissions data.
Cornell offered over six thousand waitlist spots last year. Admitted three hundred sixty-two.
Yale and Dartmouth took nobody off their waitlists.
If you're waitlisted this year, log it. Help us build the first public waitlist tracker. accepted dot fyi.
